questions.question: Explain the alarm() function and its return value.
keyPoints: alarm() schedules SIGALRM to be delivered after specified seconds
Returns number of seconds remaining from previous pending alarm
If no previous alarm, returns 0
alarm(0) cancels pending alarm

questions.question: What is the purpose of pause() function and how does it work?
keyPoints: pause() suspends process execution until signal arrives
Returns only when signal handler is invoked
Returns -1 with errno=EINTR on error
Does not accept any arguments

questions.question: Describe the three timer types used with setitimer().
keyPoints: ITIMER_REAL - real time, delivers SIGALRM
ITIMER_VIRTUAL - user mode time only, delivers SIGVTALRM
ITIMER_PROF - user+system time, delivers SIGPROF
Each has it_value (current) and it_interval (reload) components

questions.question: Explain the race condition that can occur when implementing timeout using alarm().
keyPoints: Race between checking condition and making blocking call
Signal may arrive between these two operations
If signal arrives before blocking call, handler runs but call still blocks
Solution: use sigprocmask() to block signals during critical section

questions.question: Differentiate between alarm() and setitimer().
keyPoints: alarm() provides second precision, setitimer() provides microsecond precision
alarm() is one-shot, setitimer() can be periodic via it_interval
setitimer() has three timer types vs alarm()'s single real-time timer
setitimer() uses struct itimerval, alarm() uses simple unsigned int

questions.question: What happens to pending alarm when a process calls fork() or exec()?
keyPoints: fork() - child inherits copy of pending alarm but separate timer
exec() - all pending alarms are cancelled
alarm() state is not preserved across exec()
Child gets fresh timer copy after fork()

questions.question: How can alarm() be used to implement a simple sleep() function?
keyPoints: Set alarm for desired duration using alarm()
Call pause() to wait for the signal
SIGALRM handler does nothing but return
Handle race condition by checking if already signaled
